data.title: TotalEnergies launches its largest European solar project
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said Thursday it has started up its largest solar project in Europe, a cluster of five solar projects near Sevilla in Spain with a total installed capacity of 263 MW.

The solar field will produce 515 GWh/year of renewable electricity, equivalent to the consumption of more than 150K Spanish households, and will avoid 245k tons/year of carbon emissions, TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) said, adding that most of the electricity produced will be sold through long-term power purchase agreements and the rest will be sold on the wholesale market.

The installation of 400K bifacial solar panels with trackers during construction was declared of strategic interest by the regional Andalusian government and has provided a significant economic boost to the local economy, the company said.

TotalEnergies (TTE) said it has 28 GW of gross installed renewable electricity generation capacity worldwide, with a goal of reaching 35 GW by year-end 2025, part of a strategy to expand investment in renewables alongside oil and gas.

data.title: Arrive AI secures $40 million capital infusion via Streeterville Capital
data.description:
• Arrive AI (NASDAQ:ARAI) has secured financial backing of up to $40 million from Streeterville Capital, it said on Thursday. 

  • The funding will be used for production and company growth in the US and abroad.

  • Access to the capital coincided with Arrive AI's (NASDAQ:ARAI) public debut on Nasdaq on May 15, 2025.

  • "This significant capital injection is one of the reasons we went to the public markets," said Arrive AI CEO Dan O'Toole. "We celebrate the forward thinking Streeterville is leading with in their major commitment to our vision."

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Strategy announces up to $2.1B at-the-market preferred stock offering
data.description:
Strategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) announced on Thursday a sales agreement to issue shares of its 10.00% Series A Perpetual Strife Preferred Stock (NASDAQ:STRF) worth up to $2.1B in an at-the-market offering.

MSTR shares were +1.45% to $408.52 pre-market, while STRF was -0.65% to $100.00.

The world's first Bitcoin Treasury company said it expects to make the sales in a disciplined manner over an extended period.

The net proceeds are intended to be used for general corporate purposes, including the acquisition of bitcoin, and for working capital.

data.title: Target loses its bull rating at BofA after another quarter of top-line weakness
data.description:
Bank of America downgraded Target (NYSE:TGT) to a Hold rating after having the retailer slotted with a Buy rating. Analyst Robert Ohmes and his team dramatically lowered its FY26 EPS estimate on Target (NYSE:TGT) after factoring in another quarter that showed market share losses to peers such as Walmart (WMT) and Amazon (AMZN).

"We see increased uncertainty as top-line weakness continues and the timing of comp recovery gets pushed out, with softer sales driving higher markdowns and thus incremental margin pressure for TGT," warned Ohmes.

On a broad level, the firm thinks near-term comparable sales and margin headwinds could pressure the stock despite a stronger long-term outlook supported by Target's (TGT) merchandising initiatives, growth in high-margin ancillary businesses, and opportunities for margin recovery, including from reduced inventory shrink and improving digital profitability.

BofA assigned a price objective of $105 to Target (TGT), down from its prior PO of $145.

The consensus Wall Street rating on Target (TGT) is Hold, while Seeking Alpha analysts have a consensus Buy rating on the general merchandise stock. 

Shares of Target (TGT) slipped 0.8% in premarket trading on Thursday, after shedding 5.2% on Wednesday.

data.title: Bitcoin sets new high, topping $111K on Bitcoin Pizza Day
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) forged to a new high on the 15th anniversary of the first commercial transaction that used the world's first cryptocurrency. 

The token rose as high as $111.7K Thursday morning. By 8:03 AM ET, it edged down to $111.0K. In the past five days, bitcoin (BTC-USD) gained 7.3%.

Most other popular cryptocurrencies also caught bids. Ethereum (ETH-USD) rose 4.6% to $2.65K, XRP (XRP-USD) increased 3.1% to $2.427, solana (SOL-USD) climbed 5.3% to $178.26, dogecoin (DOGE-USD) jumped 6.3% to $0.24, and cardano (ADA-USD) advanced 6.2% to $0.803.

Many bitcoin-related followed the uptrend, in contrast with the more tepid trading for the broader stock markets. S&P futures edged down 0.1%, Dow futures fell 0.2%, and Nasdaq futures were roughly flat.

Bitcoin miners were some of the biggest gainers. Greenidge Generation Holdings (GREE) surged 11%, IREN Limited (IREN) climbed 4.5%, CleanSpark (CLSK) climbed 4.2%, MARA Holdings (MARA) +3.9%, Bit Digital (BTBT) +3.8%,  Bitfarms (BITF) +3.7%, and Riot Platforms (RIOT) +3.2%.

Companies that serve the crypto space also benefited. Bakkt Holdings (BKKT) rose 4.0%, Coinbase (COIN) rose 1.9%, and Robinhood (HOOD) notched up 0.6%.

Strategy (MSTR), which holds billions of dollars of bitcoin (BTC-USD) on its balance sheeet, rose 1.4%.

data.title: Analog Devices rises after Q2 results, Q3 outlook exceed expectations
data.description:
Shares of Analog Devices (NASDAQ:ADI) climbed about 3% premarket on Thursday after second quarter results and third quarter outlook exceeded analysts' expectations.

For the second quarter of fiscal 2025 ended May 3, revenue surged about 22% year-over-year to $2.64B. Meanwhile, Adjusted EPS soared nearly 32% year-over-year to $1.85. Both top and bottom-line numbers beat estimates.

"ADI delivered second quarter revenue and earnings per share above the high end of guidance,"  said Chair and CEO Vincent Roche. "Against a backdrop of global trade volatility, our performance reflects the ongoing cyclical recovery, and the strength and resiliency of our business model."

The company said it returned $0.7B to shareholders via dividends and repurchases during the second quarter.

Analog Devices' board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.99 per share. The dividend will be paid on June 18 to all shareholders of record at the close of business on June 4.

Outlook:

"Second quarter bookings accelerated across all end markets and all regions, resulting in continued sequential backlog growth. The improving demand signals we saw throughout our fiscal Q2, support our outlook for continued growth in Q3, and reinforce our view that we are in a cyclical upturn," said CFO Richard Puccio.

For the third quarter of fiscal 2025, the company expects revenue of $2.75B, +/- $100M versus the consensus of $2.62B.

Analog Devices expects adjusted EPS to be $1.92, +/-$0.10, compared to the consensus of $1.82. 

Related stocks: Texas Instruments (TXN), Intel (INTC), Microchip Technology (MCHP), Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) were largely flat premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Ralph Lauren Non-GAAP EPS of $2.27 beats by $0.23, revenue of $1.7B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Ralph Lauren press release (NYSE:RL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.27 beats by $0.23.
  • Revenue of $1.7B (+8.3% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • Global Direct-to-Consumer Comparable Store Sales Increased 13% in the Fourth Quarter and 10% for the Full Year, Including Positive Retail Comps Across Regions and Channels and High Single-Digit Growth in Average Unit Retail ("AUR"), Demonstrating Continued Strong Pricing Power
  • The Company announced that its Board of Directors declared a 10% increase in the regular quarterly cash dividend on the Company's Common Stock. The new quarterly cash dividend is $0.9125 per share for a total annual dividend amount of $3.65 per share. The next quarterly dividend is expected to be paid on July 11, 2025 to shareholders of record at the close of business on June 27, 2025.
  • For the first quarter, the Company expects revenues to increase approximately high-single digits to last year on a constant currency basis.

  • The full year Fiscal 2026 tax rate is expected to be in the range of 20% to 22%, assuming a continuation of current tax laws. First quarter of Fiscal 2026 tax rate is expected to be approximately 20% to 21%.

    The Company is planning capital expenditures for Fiscal 2026 of approximately 4% to 5% of revenue.
